text.title: Beta Cohort Communication Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Beta Cohort Communication application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every broadcast MUST contain exactly one ask (use the new feature, reply with a use-case, fill a 2-question survey). Multiple asks dilute response rate.
rule.rationale: Email-marketing research — anchors Beta Cohort Communication rule `one-ask-per-send`.

rule.statement: All communication MUST classify as one of: weekly recap, single-ask survey, milestone announcement. Other shapes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Beta-lifecycle research — anchors Beta Cohort Communication rule `three-message-types`.

rule.statement: Cohort messages MUST tag the recipient segment (early-adopter / quiet / lapsed). Untagged sends prevent recovery flows.
rule.rationale: Lifecycle marketing — anchors Beta Cohort Communication rule `named-segment-tags`.

rule.statement: Founder MUST reply to cohort questions within 72h. Slower = cohort assumes beta is abandoned.
rule.rationale: Beta-engagement field reports — anchors Beta Cohort Communication rule `reply-sla-72h`.

rule.statement: Cohort MUST receive a graduation message (paid offer or polite sunset) at the charter's end-date. Skipping = cohort feels orphaned and stops responding.
rule.rationale: Cohort-graduation practice — anchors Beta Cohort Communication rule `graduation-message-required`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
